articles.title: MICROSOFT TEASES THE FUTURE OF WINDOWS: 'THE COMPUTER WILL BE ABLE TO SEE WHAT WE SEE, HEAR WHAT WE HEAR, AND WE CAN TALK TO IT'
articles.summary: Microsoft's VP for OS Security, David Weston, envisions Windows 2030 featuring multimodal AI interactions with voice commands. Computers will see what users see and hear what they hear, enabling sophisticated natural communication beyond traditional mouse-and-keyboard interfaces. The author questions whether such dramatic changes will realistically occur by 2030, suggesting the timeline may be overly ambitious.

articles.title: PIXEL BUDS 2A LEAK REVEALS REFRESHED DESIGN
articles.summary: Leaked renders reveal Google's redesigned Pixel Buds 2a with a refreshed case featuring unified colors matching the earbuds. The budget-friendly A-series model comes in Iris purple and other colors, including black, Hazel, and Fog Light. Leaked specifications show Active Noise Cancellation, Spatial Audio, and a seven-hour battery life ahead of the Made by Google event.

articles.title: LIQUID GLASS GETS MORE CHANGES IN IOS 26 BETA 6, HERE'S WHAT'S NEW
articles.summary: iOS 26 beta 6 brings refined Liquid Glass visuals, including a more transparent Lock Screen clock that changes appearance with wallpaper, improved tab bar text legibility, and expanded chromatic effects for UI elements like toggles and tab bars. While no major design reversals are expected before launch, these tweaks make the new look feel more polished.

articles.title: OUTCOMES OVER OUTPUT: DESIGN SYSTEMS AND AI
articles.summary: Design system practitioners surveyed show cautious optimism about AI's impact, rating their future confidence 3.3 out of 5. Leadership seeks quick AI integration to avoid falling behind, but practitioners emphasize gaps between AI hype and practical workflow improvements. Design systems should serve as AI training infrastructure rather than focusing on output, requiring evolution toward strategic roles.

articles.title: IT'S 2025. WHY DOES UX STILL SUCK?
articles.summary: Digital user experiences remain fundamentally flawed despite decades of research and established best practices. Three potential causes include underinvestment in UX, inexperienced practitioners, and talent being excluded from critical technical decisions. A trillion-dollar opportunity awaits companies willing to differentiate through genuinely delightful user experiences.

articles.title: CONFESSIONS FROM A SELL OUT DESIGNER
articles.summary: A designer's perspective shifted from pure user advocacy to embracing business priorities such as ROI, strategic focus, scope control, technical feasibility, and balancing speed with quality. The evolution reframed “selling out” as learning the business's language. It enabled the designer to gain influence, secure resources, and deliver greater impact for both users and the organization.

articles.title: 2D LOGO TO 3D ROTATING ANIMATION IN SECONDS
articles.summary: Turn your 2D logo into a 3D animation in seconds. Add chrome effects, spin it 360°, and export as a video or 3D model.

articles.title: AI-POWERED PHOTO EDITING STUDIO FOR MARKETING
articles.summary: Explore X-Design's photo editing workspace to create professional photos in minutes. Whether you are resizing images, adjusting colors, or adding transparent backgrounds to photos, X-Design makes it easy to edit images like a pro.

articles.title: BUILD COMPLETE APPS BY MESSAGING AI
articles.summary: Turn your ideas into applications in minutes. Create a revenue-ready product by chatting with AI.

articles.title: IN USER RESEARCH, DON'T STOP AT “YES” OR “NO”
articles.summary: Product stakeholders frequently seek binary validation from user research rather than using it for discovery and insight generation. Simple yes/no responses prove unreliable and misleading, as participants often provide socially desirable answers without genuine feedback. Research delivers greater value when capturing nuanced insights about solution strengths and weaknesses rather than just flat validation.

articles.title: AI WON'T KILL UX, OUR HABITS WILL: WHY DESIGNERS MUST STAY INTENTIONAL
articles.summary: AI tools aren't destroying UX design—designers' unintentional habits of prioritizing speed over thoughtful, human-centered processes are the real threat. Machine learning replicates existing patterns rather than innovating, creating efficient but soulless experiences when designers rely on automation without reflection. Maintaining curiosity, intentional research practices, and human empathy remains crucial as teams must use AI to support rather than replace critical thinking.

articles.title: STUDIO KILN REFRESHES VINTED'S BRAND WITH A CONFIDENT, FLEXIBLE IDENTITY ROOTED IN ITS COMMUNITY SPIRIT
articles.summary: Vinted's refreshed identity, developed with Studio Kiln, modernises and unifies the brand while preserving its recognisable handwritten logo and signature Calypso green. The update introduces a flowing line graphic, refined colour palette with added Mint accents, custom VC Honey typography, tactile imagery, and motion design, creating a flexible, globally adaptable toolkit that strengthens brand recognition, celebrates its circular economy mission, and stands out in a competitive resale market.

articles.title: THE BRUTAL REASONS WHY CLIENTS STOP HIRING YOU (AND HOW TO FIX IT)
articles.summary: Poor communication, unreliability, complacency, and the loss of personal connections drive clients to seek freelance replacements.

articles.title: ALYSHA TAKOUSHIAN ON NATURE, NOSTALGIA, AND NURTURING CREATIVITY THROUGH ILLUSTRATION
articles.summary: Alysha Takoushian is a Copenhagen-based illustrator whose lifelong passion for drawing blends nature-inspired observation, analogue craft, and teaching, using art as both a creative practice and a way to nurture curiosity, connection, and gratitude.
